weapons.name: Dull Aegis Blade
weapons.description: A rusted blade that feels strangely heavy, as if weighted by forgotten years.
weapons.lore: The original weapon of the Archive, now stripped of its history.

weapons.name: Refined Aegis Blade
weapons.description: The rust has flaked away, revealing shimmering silver filigree.

mechanics.ability: Memory Echo: Chance to deal double damage on third strike.

weapons.progression: Requires Shard of Courage and 500 Essence.

mnemurgy_tools.name: The Memory Lantern
mnemurgy_tools.description: A blue-flamed lantern that reveals things that aren't there anymore.

mechanics.active: Reveal hidden platforms and invisible 'Ink-Born' enemies within a small radius.

mnemurgy_tools.lore: Elara's gift. It burns using the 'will to know'.

mnemurgy_tools.name: Compassion Pulse
mnemurgy_tools.description: A resonant stone that emits a wave of empathetic energy.

mechanics.active: Heals wilted environmental hazards (brambles) and stuns 'Hollow' enemies.

mnemurgy_tools.progression: Unlocked after retrieving the Shard of Empathy.

mnemurgy_tools.name: The Tether of Regret
mnemurgy_tools.description: A spectral chain that can latch onto '錨' (Anchor) points.

mechanics.active: Pulls Kaelen to distant hooks or pulls light enemies toward him.

mnemurgy_tools.lore: A physical manifestation of the ties that bind the past to the present.

key_artifacts.name: Shard of Identity
key_artifacts.description: A prismatic fragment that hums with the sound of a thousand names.
key_artifacts.impact: Unlocks basic combat skills and the ability to interact with Oakhaven NPCs.

key_artifacts.name: Fragment of Vitality
key_artifacts.mechanics: Collecting 4 increases Max Health by 1 Heart.

key_artifacts.name: The Forbidden Tome
key_artifacts.description: A book whose pages are made of pure shadow.
key_artifacts.impact: Required to enter the Citadel of Erasure; reveals Malakor's true origin.

consumables.name: Condensed Thought
consumables.description: A shimmering liquid that tastes like a childhood summer.
